methods.json.method: Collection-scoped overrides via body collection ID
methods.json.description: Apply page-specific styling by scoping rules to the Squarespace body ID `#collection-...` so changes don't leak across the site.
methods.json.how_it_works: Squarespace sets `body#collection-<id>` per page. Custom SCSS nests page-specific rules under that ID (e.g., header padding adjustments and decorative pseudo-elements).

methods.json.method: Section-specific styling via custom section IDs
methods.json.description: Use short, human-assigned section IDs (e.g., `#t-1`, `#a-1`, `#c-1`) as stable hooks for deep layout overrides.
methods.json.how_it_works: Squarespace sections can be assigned an ID in the editor. SCSS targets that ID to reshape internal wrappers like `.content-wrapper`, `.section-border`, `.section-background`, and grid rows/columns.

methods.json.method: Half-and-half section layout by splitting `.content-wrapper` vs `.section-border` widths
methods.json.description: Create asymmetric split layouts by setting `.content-wrapper` to one width and `.section-border` (and its background) to the remainder, using margins to push each side into position.
methods.json.how_it_works: Within a section ID scope, `.content-wrapper` gets explicit width (e.g., 50–60%) and opposing margin (e.g., `margin-right: 50%`) while `.section-border` gets the complementary width and offset (e.g., `margin-left: 50%`).

methods.json.method: Decorative rails using pseudo-elements anchored to collection scope
methods.json.description: Inject fixed decorative images (left/right bars) without adding markup by using `:before`/`:after` on the page collection wrapper.
methods.json.how_it_works: Within `#collection-...`, define `&:before` and `&:after` as absolutely positioned blocks with `background-image` pointing to Squarespace-hosted assets; high `z-index` keeps them above content.

methods.json.method: Global parallax on body pseudo-elements using CSS variables + rAF scroll loop
methods.json.description: Create subtle background motion by transforming `body:before`/`body:after` with CSS variables updated on scroll.
methods.json.how_it_works: CSS sets `transform: translate3d(0, var(--before-parallax), 0)` on `body:before, body:after`. JS uses `requestAnimationFrame` to update `--before-parallax` and `--after-parallax` based on scrollY with configurable speeds.
evidence: site_scss.scss: body:before, body:after { transform: translate3d(0, var(--before-parallax, 0px), 0); ... }

methods.json.description: Ensure custom JS runs both on first load and after in-site navigation by binding to Squarespace's `mercury:load` event.
methods.json.how_it_works: A single `init()` function is attached to `DOMContentLoaded` and `window.addEventListener('mercury:load', init)`; inside, features are gated by element existence checks.

methods.json.method: Feature gating by section presence checks
methods.json.description: Only run feature logic on pages/sections that include the required DOM nodes.
methods.json.how_it_works: Use jQuery `if ($('#<id>').length) { ... }` to conditionally bind listeners or initialize plugins, preventing errors on other pages.

methods.json.method: SVG animation driven by scroll transform on internal SVG parts
methods.json.description: Animate SVG components by rotating specific SVG element IDs in response to scroll position.
methods.json.how_it_works: Select the SVG root (`#cogs`) and specific sub-elements (`#top`, `#bottom`, `#center`). On scroll, set `style.transform = rotate(...)` for each piece; CSS ensures correct `transform-box` and `transform-origin`.
evidence: site_js.js: const svg = document.querySelector('#cogs'); const cog1 = svg.querySelector('#top'); ... window.addEventListener('scroll', ... rotate ...)

methods.json.method: Marquee-style gallery carousel via Slick with linear autoplay
methods.json.description: Convert a Squarespace gallery into a continuously scrolling strip using Slick carousel settings.
methods.json.how_it_works: On `#t-3`, initialize `$('#t-3 .sqs-gallery').slick({ autoplay: true, autoplaySpeed: 0, speed: 5000, cssEase: 'linear', arrows:false, dots:false, responsive:[...] })` to mimic a marquee.
evidence: site_js.js: if($('#t-3').length){ $('#t-3 .sqs-gallery').slick({ autoplaySpeed: 0, speed: 5000, cssEase: 'linear', ... responsive breakpoint 900 }) }
site_js.js: includes slick-carousel CDN script/link references

methods.json.method: Single-block surgical fixes using generated `#block-yui_...` IDs
methods.json.description: Apply one-off layout fixes by targeting Squarespace's generated block IDs when no better stable hook exists.
methods.json.how_it_works: Use the block's unique ID selector to override padding/layout for that exact block only (e.g., applying flex to a code container).

methods.json.method: Preload/intro timing via delayed body state class
methods.json.description: Coordinate CSS-driven intro states by adding a `loaded` class to the body after a short delay.
methods.json.how_it_works: JS runs `setTimeout(() => $('body').addClass('loaded'), 800)`; CSS can key transitions off `body.loaded` (not shown in snippets, but implied by the pattern).

css-patterns.json.rule: When a change is page-specific (header spacing, decorative overlays), it is nested under the page collection ID to avoid global side effects.

css-patterns.json.pattern: Section hooks use short semantic IDs
css-patterns.json.rule: Custom section IDs follow a compact prefix-number scheme (`a-*`, `t-*`, `c-*`, `l-*`) and serve as the primary layout override points.

css-patterns.json.pattern: Squarespace wrapper reshaping instead of adding markup
css-patterns.json.rule: Layout is achieved by overriding Squarespace structural wrappers (`.content-wrapper`, `.section-border`, `.section-background`) rather than relying on custom HTML structure.

css-patterns.json.pattern: Split layout uses complementary widths + opposing margins
css-patterns.json.rule: Two-panel sections are formed by explicit width splits (50/50, 60/40) and margin offsets (left/right) applied to `.content-wrapper` and `.section-border`.

css-patterns.json.pattern: Decoration via pseudo-elements with hosted assets
css-patterns.json.rule: Background decorations are frequently injected with `:before`/`:after` blocks and `background-image` URLs pointing to Squarespace static assets.

css-patterns.json.pattern: Heavy use of `!important` to defeat theme defaults
css-patterns.json.rule: Overrides often append `!important` on padding/margins/min-height/backgrounds to out-prioritize Squarespace and template styles.
Mobile/tablet adjustments are clustered around a `max-width: 900px` breakpoint for typography and layout constraints.

css-patterns.json.pattern: Grid-to-flex conversion inside `.row` for alignment control
css-patterns.json.rule: When Squarespace grid behavior is limiting, `.row` is converted to flex and child spans are aligned/weighted via nested selectors.
Repeated elements are auto-numbered using `:nth-of-type` selectors that set `content: '1'`, `content: '2'`, etc.

css-patterns.json.pattern: Custom typography via @font-face from Squarespace static URLs
css-patterns.json.rule: Brand fonts are self-hosted on Squarespace and registered through multiple `@font-face` declarations.

css-patterns.json.pattern: Global motion uses CSS variables on body pseudo-elements
css-patterns.json.rule: A single global parallax system is expressed in CSS as transforms on `body:before` and `body:after` driven by CSS variables.

js-patterns.json.pattern: Dual lifecycle init (first load + SPA navigation)
js-patterns.json.rule: Always bind initialization to both `DOMContentLoaded` and Squarespace `mercury:load`.
js-patterns.json.trigger: Page load or in-site navigation that triggers `mercury:load`

js-patterns.json.rule: Wrap every feature in an element existence check keyed to section IDs to avoid cross-page runtime errors.
js-patterns.json.trigger: Presence of a section hook like `#a-3` or `#t-3` in the DOM

js-patterns.json.pattern: Slick carousel used as a marquee strip
js-patterns.json.rule: Initialize Slick with `autoplaySpeed: 0`, long `speed`, and `cssEase: 'linear'` for continuous scrolling.
js-patterns.json.trigger: Presence of `#t-3` (and its `.sqs-gallery`) on the page

js-patterns.json.pattern: Scroll-driven animation via direct style transforms
js-patterns.json.rule: Use `window.addEventListener('scroll', ...)` to set transforms on key elements (SVG parts) based on `window.scrollY`.
js-patterns.json.trigger: Presence of `#a-3` (and SVG `#cogs`) on the page

js-patterns.json.rule: On scroll, throttle updates with a `ticking` flag and update CSS variables inside `requestAnimationFrame`.
js-patterns.json.trigger: Any scroll event after the parallax IIFE runs

js-patterns.json.pattern: CSS-state timing via delayed body class
js-patterns.json.rule: Use a fixed timeout to add a `loaded` class for coordinating CSS transitions with perceived load timing.

squarespace-rules.json: Assign explicit section IDs in Squarespace (e.g., a-*, t-*, c-*) and treat them as the primary long-lived styling/JS hooks.
Keep page-specific changes scoped under `body#collection-<id>` to prevent accidental global bleed.
Prefer overriding Squarespace structural wrappers (`.content-wrapper`, `.section-border`, `.section-background`) rather than adding heavy custom HTML.
Use pseudo-elements for decorative graphics to avoid extra blocks/markup and keep the editor content clean.
When Squarespace’s grid constraints get in the way, convert `.row` containers to flex inside section scopes for precise alignment.
For SPA reliability in Squarespace 7.1, bind JS init to both `DOMContentLoaded` and `mercury:load`, and gate features by DOM presence checks.
Use generated block IDs (`#block-yui_...`) only for truly one-off fixes where no stable semantic hook exists.

layout-strategies.json.strategy: Split-panel sections using native section wrappers
layout-strategies.json.description: Construct two-column/dual-surface sections by treating `.content-wrapper` and `.section-border` as independent panels with complementary widths and offsets.
techniques: Section-specific styling via custom section IDs
Half-and-half section layout by splitting `.content-wrapper` vs `.section-border` widths

layout-strategies.json.description: Add site/page framing elements (side rails, overlays) through `:before`/`:after` on collection-scoped selectors, keeping the CMS content minimal.
techniques: Decorative rails using pseudo-elements anchored to collection scope
Collection-scoped overrides via body collection ID

layout-strategies.json.strategy: Global motion layer driven by CSS variables
layout-strategies.json.description: Create a lightweight parallax/motion system by animating `body` pseudo-elements with CSS variables updated via rAF on scroll.
techniques: Global parallax on body pseudo-elements using CSS variables + rAF scroll loop

layout-strategies.json.strategy: Interactive accents tied to section presence
layout-strategies.json.description: Attach animation and plugin behaviors only where needed by gating JS on section IDs, enabling reuse of a single site-wide script safely.
techniques: Feature gating by section presence checks
SVG animation driven by scroll transform on internal SVG parts
Marquee-style gallery carousel via Slick with linear autoplay

layout-strategies.json.strategy: Override Squarespace grid behavior with scoped flex
layout-strategies.json.description: Within targeted sections, convert grid rows to flex for bottom alignment, asymmetric padding, and custom column sizing beyond template defaults.
techniques: Section-specific styling via custom section IDs
Grid-to-flex conversion inside `.row` for alignment control

key_techniques: Collection-scoped overrides via body collection ID
Global parallax on body pseudo-elements using CSS variables + rAF scroll loop
home.json.why_it_matters: Home establishes the global runtime patterns (init lifecycle + parallax variables) that can be reused site-wide without coupling to a single page.

key_techniques: Section-specific styling via custom section IDs
Feature gating by section presence checks
notable_patterns: Section id hooks (e.g., a-1) exist in markup and are expected in SCSS/JS gating
about.json.why_it_matters: About demonstrates the core approach: editorial sections are given stable IDs and then reshaped by targeting Squarespace wrapper elements.

key_techniques: Half-and-half section layout by splitting `.content-wrapper` vs `.section-border` widths
Decorative rails using pseudo-elements anchored to collection scope
Marquee-style gallery carousel via Slick with linear autoplay
notable_patterns: Sections include id="t-1" and related t-* hooks used in SCSS/JS
team.json.why_it_matters: Team is the clearest example of the methodology combining: page-scoped decoration, section-scoped layout overrides, and section-gated JS plugins.

key_techniques: Half-and-half section layout by splitting `.content-wrapper` vs `.section-border` widths
Grid-to-flex conversion inside `.row` for alignment control
notable_patterns: Sections include id="c-1" / "c-2" hooks referenced in SCSS
contact.json.why_it_matters: Contact highlights the practice of making complex layouts from Squarespace’s native wrappers instead of custom markup, keeping the editor structure simple.
